projection_metadata.base_year_source: UNESCAP Official / Ministry of Road & Transport

low.label: Low Growth (Conservative)
low.assumptions: Policy neutral, import constraints remain, limited charging infrastructure expansion
low.drivers: Weak policy incentives, price barriers, infrastructure gaps
low.notes: Favors modular infrastructure; reduces capital risk

base.label: Base Case (Moderate)
base.assumptions: Current policy continues, moderate charging infrastructure growth, stable import increase
base.drivers: Policy continuity, gradual infrastructure development, moderate price competition
base.notes: Central scenario for planning; triggers phased approach

high.label: High Growth (Accelerated)
high.assumptions: Government incentives, EV price drop due to competition, rapid urban charging expansion
high.drivers: Strong policy support, price reduction, charging network build-out, corporate adoption
high.notes: Creates aggressive waste timeline; requires early infrastructure deployment

strategic_implications.low_scenario_impact: → Delays battery waste emergence by 2-3 years → Favors modular infrastructure → Extended investment window
strategic_implications.base_scenario_impact: → Moderate waste surge post-2031 → 1,500t hub viable 2031-2032 → Scale decision gate 2033-2035
strategic_implications.high_scenario_impact: → Earlier, steeper waste curve → Full-scale plant consideration post-2033 → Urgent policy & supply chain work
